feat(topology): 4-node federation mesh + homogeneous agent groups

Four coordinated changes addressing operator feedback:

## 1. ai-memory on EVERY node, W=2 of N=4 federation

Every droplet now runs ai-memory serve with
`--quorum-writes 2 --quorum-peers <other 3>`. Agents talk to their
LOCAL ai-memory on 127.0.0.1:9077. Federation handles replication
across the mesh. Node-4 remains a dedicated memory-only peer so
scenarios can inspect cluster state independently of any agent path.

## 2. Homoge

fix: stop tracking dev.db in git, use Actions Cache for persistence

prisma/dev.db grew to 103MB exceeding GitHub's 100MB limit.
DB is now persisted via actions/cache between workflow runs.
Only src/data/ JSON files are committed to the repo.

fix(prune): don't delete runtime .js modules with changelog-prefix names

prune-clawdbot's startsWith('changelog') prefix rule was deleting
changelog.js from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/utils/, causing
ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND at gateway startup after install.

Restrict the rule to non-runtime files only (anything that isn't
.js/.cjs/.mjs). Applied inside the pruneArtifacts() refactor from main.

Tool-aware personas tightly coupled to the new WordPress (19 tools)
and Shopify (14 tools) MCP skills — each persona references the exact
skill tool names, API semantics (WP post lifecycle, Shopify GIDs +

sub-agent can act decisively without re-deriving patterns per call.

feat(coding): add pure GitContextResolver module (#569 PR 1/8) (#577)

* feat(coding): add pure GitContextResolver module (#569 PR 1/8)

Introduces `packages/remnic-core/src/coding/git-context.ts`, a pure
detection module that returns `{ projectId, branch, rootPath,
defaultBranch }` for a given cwd inside a git worktree, or `null` when
the cwd is outside a repo / git is unavailable.

This is slice 1 of issue #569. It ships no wiring to the orchestrator,
namespace resolver, or config — downstream

Fix skill name generation: short imperative slugs, not sentence fragments

Core problems fixed:
- _slugify was taking first 10 words of the condition sentence, producing names like
  'when-a-batch-visualization-or-plotting-process-appears-to-have'
- LLM prompts said 'be specific', causing domain-locked overly-specific conditions

Changes:
- Add SHORT_NAME field to all LLM prompts (both _refine_skill_draft and _llm_distill_single)
- Prompts now explicitly require imperative verb slugs: 'verify-X'

Production callback was returning {statusCode:500, message:"Internal
server error"} on every GitHub connect attempt. Log analysis:

    at GitHubAdapter.exchangeCodeForTokens (github.adapter.ts:217)
    at WorkspaceConnectorService.handleOAuthCallback (...)

Two root causes:

   request and returns HTTP 400 without one. Th

fix: preserve whitespace-only streaming chunks (newlines were dropped)

Checking 'chunk.Content != ""' dropped chunks that were just newlines
or spaces. This caused text like 'let me look at this:\n' to arrive as
two chunks — 'let me look at this:' followed by a dropped '\n' —
then the next sentence ran straight on with no separation.

Fix: check len(content) > 0 instead of != "" (same semantics in Go
but makes the intent explicit) and add a comment explaining why we must
not filter whitespace-o

async handles (chat-service + workspace-service both print "Force
exiting Jest" warnings locally). On slower github-actions runners the
leaked handles can prevent jest from exiting within the default window.

Adds forceExit: true and testTimeout: 30_000 to all 16 jest configs.
Does not mask test failures — jest only force-exits after the test
results are reported.

yaml.NewDecoder.Decode returns io.EOF. Previously this would result in
a confusing error 'invalid elasticclaw-config.yaml: EOF'. Now we treat

and produce the clearer error 'must specify a provider'.

fix: stop blocking Bun's event loop during Copier template creation
The POST /api/copier/create handler called `uvx copier copy` via synchronous
execSync, which froze the event loop for the entire 30–120s the command
takes (especially during uvx's first-run copier download). With the loop
blocked, Bun couldn't flush HTTP keep-alives or ship the response; the
browser eventually gave up and surfaced "NetworkError when attempting to
fetch resource" in the Template tab's red banner.

fix(dashboard): drop permission() on /api/auth/logout + /api/auth/me (H1)

These are auth-flow endpoints registered BEFORE the global auth middleware
(which is intentional, so /api/auth/login can remain public). Consequence:
c.get("user") is undefined on these routes, and permission() 401s every
request — caught by pnpm test:e2e on CI.

Semantic fix: like /api/auth/login, /logout and /me are part of the auth
mechanism itself — they establish or report identity. They validate the
caller inline (s

fix(create): preserve escaped quotes in --from-json description (#18)

Closes #15

`clawflows create --from-json` used grep/sed with `[^"]*` to extract each
JSON string field. That character class stops at the first `"` it sees,
including an escaped `\"`. A description like

at `Log food with ` and everything after was silently lost. No error was
shown. The WORKFLOW.md ended up with a truncated body.

fix(tools): rename instacart.* -> instacart_* (OpenAI name regex)

OpenAI's tool-calling API requires function names to match
^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$. Dots are rejected, so providers (including openrouter
-> openai/gpt-4o-mini) returned HTTP 400 "Invalid tools[N].function.name"
whenever the instacart-browser plugin was active. The error blocked the
entire tool payload, not just our tools — so agents couldn't do anything.

## Summary
Post-deploy SSH examples in the docs told users to \`cd
/home/ubuntu/nemoclaw\`, which fails on Brev providers that use
non-ubuntu usernames (e.g., \`shadeform\` on shadecloud). Swaps in
\`~/nemoclaw\` so the remote shell expands \`\$HOME\` to whatever the
provider assigns.

fix(cli): remove silent fallback in ACTION_TO_STAGE lookup (Cursor Bugbot PR #18)

bin/trackly cmdUpdateStatus used `ACTION_TO_STAGE[action] || action` which would
silently pass an unmapped action through as the stage value if the map ever
drifted from the command dispatcher. Mirror the defensive pattern already in
mcp/server.js: fail loud with an internal-error message rather than POSTing an
unintended stage (e.g. stage='snoozed' when backend allowlist is applied/backlog/
discarded).

feat: 新增 OpenClawCallbackPool，把会话池暴露成带反向事件回调的第三方库

- (user, session) ↔ sessionKey 的双向映射，支持自定义 sessionKey
- 所有 gateway 事件反向解析回 (user, session) 并回调 callback_handler
- 按 handler 返回值做短路处理：
    * None / ("pass", _)   默认流程，事件累积参与 reply 抽取
    * ("used", None)       事件已消费，不累积，继续等待
    * ("modify", data)     立即结束，返回 data
    * ("async", id)        立即结束，返回 {"jobid": id}
    * ("fail", errmsg)     立即结束，返回错误信息
- handler 自身异常统一转成 ("fail", ...)，不冒泡到调用者
- CallbackOutcome.to_dict() 把结果规整成 HTTP/RPC 友好的 dict

Harden MCP endpoint against cross-tenant data leak

Three layers of defense plus an audit log, all applied at the /mcp
boundary so prompt injection in agent-readable content (notes, emails,
SMS bodies) cannot exfiltrate data across tenants.

  1. Shared secret (x-mcp-secret header) on every /mcp request, checked
     with crypto.timingSafeEqual. OpenClaw config sends it via the
     register script; without it the API returns 401.
  2. HMAC-signed context token minted per chat in server/openclaw

v0.2.10: route all outbound to account.sessionKey

Cron delivery was writing assistant messages with session_key=<userId>
(from the auto-inferred delivery.to), which created a ghost session
separate from the main chat thread. iOS only loads messages for a
given session_id, so cron replies never surfaced in-app even though
push notifications fired. Onepilot is one-agent-one-thread; ignore
ctx.to and always use account.sessionKey.

fix(executor-dbos): make DBOSExecutor deep-copy-safe (#48)

After moving the message buffer onto a per-instance ContextVar,
every tool call that passed a ToolContext to execute_tool started
failing with:

    TypeError: cannot pickle '_contextvars.ContextVar' object

because ``execute_tool`` calls ``dataclasses.asdict(ctx)`` for
step-argument serialization, and asdict deep-copies every field
including ``ToolContext.executor``. The singleton executor's
ContextVar attribute is not deep-copyable.

dropping 2816 KV blocks + re-prefilling 58K tokens every request
when prompt length landed between captured SSM checkpoints.

VMLX_ENABLE_SSM_PREFIX_RESUME=1 opt-in; LLM scheduler hybrid fast
path didn't wire it at all.

feat(telegram): hot-reload new bot accounts via POST /reload

Adding a new Telegram bot token to agentx.json used to require a full
daemon restart before the new account would start polling. Now the
reload path diffs the live account map, starts pollers for added
accounts, stops pollers for removed ones, and restarts pollers for
accounts whose token rotated. Allowlist changes flow through
effectiveAllowFrom so they don't need a restart at all.

Show unread total in browser tab title

Tab title now follows Feishu / Lark convention: `<site name> (N)` when
there are unread messages across all chats, plain `<site name>` when
the total hits zero. N is capped at "99+" to match the sidebar badge.
The update is driven off `renderChatList()`, which is already the
single chokepoint for every unread mutation path (initial fetch,
`unread_updated` socket push, active-chat clear, new message on an
inactive chat), so no extra hooks are needed.

fix: per-entry wired-marker filter + cleaner host selectors + LinkedIn indent

Real bug found while investigating "no button in Outlook": the
:not([data-defluff-wired]) suffix was applied to the FULL
bodySelector string once. In CSS grammar that scopes it to the last
comma-entry only, so earlier entries (like the new
[id^="UniqueMessageBody"] Outlook selector) would re-match wired
elements on every MutationObserver tick. The `id` selector was
racing the marker on every scan.

## Summary
Local NIM onboarding fails at the image pull step because `docker pull
nvcr.io/nim/...` requires NGC registry authentication. This adds an NGC
API key prompt during onboard that runs `docker login nvcr.io
--password-stdin` before pulling the NIM image. The key is masked during
input and handled securely via stdin.

Based on the investigation in PR #236.

fix: convert start.bat to CRLF line endings and add .gitattributes

Windows cmd.exe requires CRLF (\\r\\n) line endings for batch files.
The start.bat was committed with Unix LF-only endings, which causes
it to silently malfunction when double-clicked on Windows —
especially around parenthesized if-blocks and delayed expansion.

Also adds .gitattributes so git always checks out .bat files with
CRLF and .sh files with LF, regardless of contributor platform.

fix(rebuild): restore policy presets after sandbox rebuild (#1952) (#2026)

## Summary

After `nemoclaw <name> rebuild --yes`, previously applied network policy
presets (e.g., telegram, npm) were lost because they live in the gateway
policy engine, not the sandbox filesystem. The rebuild flow recreated
the sandbox with the base policy, silently dropping all preset-applied
rules. This caused messaging bridges to fail with 403 Forbidden.

## Related Issue

Fixes #1952

fix(onboard): use Bearer auth when probing Gemini OpenAI-compat endpoint (#2094)

## Summary

The Gemini OpenAI-compat probe endpoint at `/v1beta/openai/` requires
`Authorization: Bearer <KEY>` and rejects `?key=<KEY>` with HTTP 400
"Missing or invalid Authorization header." The onboarder currently sends
`?key=` for Gemini, which blocks every Gemini onboarding run. This
change makes `getProbeAuthMode` return `undefined` for every provider so
probes default to Bearer auth.

## Related Issue

Fixe

Fix guided X sync failure on fresh deployments

Root causes:
1. owner.twitter_handle=null in agency-identity.json was never surfaced
   as an explicit blocker — sync silently failed or gave misleading status
2. Bookmarker feed parser only accepted {posts,items,data} envelopes but
   live API returns {tweets:[...]} — causing schema_mismatch/feed_size=0
3. No guided manifest producer existed — consumer-only architecture
4. Bootstrap health and feed-parse health were conflated

fix(install.ps1): make iex-from-web actually work

Two problems were biting us when users loaded install.ps1 via
`iex (iwr ...).Content`:

1. The `<# .SYNOPSIS ... #>` block-comment contained the string
   "#Requires" as prose. When iex evaluates a string (not a file), the
   block-comment boundary got confused (likely because the leading BOM
   broke tokenization) and PS treated the `#Requires` inside the
   comment as a real directive, then complained about its format.

2. The UTF-8 BOM itself

fix(install.ps1): drop #Requires so iex-from-web doesn't choke on BOM

When install.ps1 is loaded via `iex (iwr ...)`, the UTF-8 BOM ends up
inside the in-memory string and prefixes the first line. PowerShell
then sees "﻿#Requires" (with the BOM char) and reports
CommandNotFoundException. The script still runs because the rest
parses fine, but the error is alarming to first-time users.

#Requires is not critical (modern Windows ships PS 5.1), so drop it.
The BOM itself stays — without it PS 5.1

fix(install.sh): make interactive prompts work under curl | bash

When the script was piped from curl, stdin was the pipe (not a tty) so
read -p returned instantly with empty input. The while-loops spun
silently and downstream commands tripped parse errors. Now we exec <
/dev/tty when stdin is not a terminal, with a clear error if even that
is unavailable.

Also replace bash-4-only lowercase expansion `${VAR,,}` with a portable
to_lower helper via tr, so the script runs on older bash (macOS 3.x)

fix(scripts): prevent gh-as-user.sh from using bot token via gh wrapper (#47)

## Summary

- Fixes `gh-as-user.sh` finding the `gh-with-token-refresh.sh` wrapper
instead of the real `gh` binary when `GH_AUTH_MODE=app`
- Adds `GH_TOKEN_FILE` to the list of unset env vars so no wrapper can
re-inject the bot token

## Root Cause

When `GH_AUTH_MODE=app`, `lib-auth.sh` prepends the dispatcher's
`scripts/` directory to `PATH` and symlinks `gh` →
`gh-with-token-refresh.sh` there. `gh-as-user.sh` only

* fix(executor): make DirectExecutor's message buffer per-task via ContextVar

`DirectExecutor._messages` was a plain instance attribute, and the
executor itself is a process-wide singleton wired at app startup. Two
concurrent turns running through `_run_agent_loop` (e.g. a cron firing
while an IPC turn is in flight) trampled each other's buffer: one
turn's `set_messages` reset the list, the peer's next `load_messages`
returned the wron

fix(chunker): pass allowedSpecial via encode options object (#5)

gpt-tokenizer's encode() signature is encode(text, options?) where
options is an object with an allowedSpecial field. Passing "all" as
the second positional argument is silently treated as an options
object with no matching keys, so special tokens remain disallowed
and encode() still throws on input containing <|endoftext|>.

Correct form: encode(text, { allowedSpecial: "all" }).

fix(policy): restore L4 tunnel for WebSocket hosts via tls: skip (#2098)

OpenShell [v0.0.15+ auto-terminates TLS
unconditionally](https://github.com/NVIDIA/OpenShell/pull/544) on every
detected TLS stream, which also applies to WSS. Without an explicit
opt-out, messaging WebSocket endpoints (previously shipping as pure L4
CONNECT tunnels via `access: full`) are now MITMed, and the Discord
client consistently loses its gateway connection with close code 1006.

Add `tls: skip` to the affected end

fix(blob): drop client-side x-amz-checksum-sha256 header on PUTs

S3 rejected the PUTs with 'headers not signed: x-amz-checksum-sha256' because the presigned URL (from the gateway) didn't include that header in its signed headers list. The gateway now signs URLs without ChecksumAlgorithm (see run402-private commit d1c0f774), so the client must NOT send the checksum header either.

Expands the SKILL.md from "one email → bullets" to three modes:

- Single email (current behavior) — 3-5 bullets in priority order
  (actions → questions → facts → intent).

fix: apply backoff when topic repeat retry also repeats

When a thought topic is detected as a repeat, Soul retries with a
different opportunity type. If the retry also repeats, it now calls
applySkipBackoff() to break the infinite loop instead of returning
without any backoff, which caused the same topic to repeat every tick.

fix(file-tree): invalidate both cached target and ancestor, skip collapsed refetches

When a deep directory was renamed or deleted externally and both the
directory itself and a parent directory were cached, only the self entry
was cleared — leaving the parent's children list holding a ghost reference
to the old child. Walk up to the nearest cached ancestor in addition to
the direct hit so both stale entries are dropped together.

refactor: heuristic only commits on explicit switches, escalates everything else

Architectural shift: split the heuristic's two jobs. It now detects
stay vs escalate (binary), but does NOT pick which thread to switch to
on keyword evidence. Picking is the LLM's job.

Why: keyword-based picking produced high-confidence wrong routes that
bypassed the LLM tier. The worst failure mode was "confident wrong
commit" rather than "uncertain escalation". Real dogfood feedback

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD041 -->
## Summary

`policy-list` previously read only from CLI-side registry metadata
(`~/.nemoclaw/sandboxes.json`), not from the gateway's actual loaded
policy. After `rebuild`, this caused presets to show as ○ (not applied)
while the gateway still enforced them. This PR adds gateway
cross-referencing so `policy-list` shows the true state of both sources.

The Hetzner deploy script had accumulated three concentric workarounds
that each introduced its own failure mode:

     an IPv4/IPv6 dual-stack trap (systemd's numeric-port shortcut
     set IPV6_V6ONLY=1, leaving port 22 IPv4-unreachable — cost us an
     hour of debugging today).
  2. fail2ban whitelist on a runtime-detected public IP. Auto-discover

fix: suppress PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning from asyncio subprocess GC

Root cause: Python 3.12's BaseSubprocessTransport.__del__ fires during
garbage collection after event loop closes. This is a known CPython issue
in async subprocess tests, not a code bug. The filterwarnings config in
pyproject.toml suppresses this specific pytest warning class.

The ssh.socket drop-in used:
  [Socket]
  ListenStream=
  ListenStream=22
  ListenStream=222

which on Hetzner CAX11 (Ubuntu 24.04.3, systemd 255) created an
IPv6-ONLY socket. `ss -tlnp` showed only `[::]:22`, no `0.0.0.0:22`.
IPv4 clients got "Connection refused" because the kernel had no
v4 listener. This surprised me because the system-wide sysctl
`net.ipv6.bindv6only = 0` should make [::]:22 accept v4 via
mapped-v6 — but syste

fix: prevent crash loop from unhandled promise rejections in watch renewal (#1)

setTimeout(async () => { await renewFn() }) produces an unhandled promise
rejection when renewFn throws (e.g. expired OAuth token). Node crashes,
Railway restarts, the watch timer fires again — infinite crash loop.

- mail/watch.ts: replace async setTimeout callback with .then/.catch pattern
- calendar/watch.ts: add try/catch around renewFn().catch() for sync throws
- mail/auth.ts, calendar/auth.ts: clear auth cache

fix(billing): retry stuck INAPP top-up purchases on app startup (#1908)

If verify-google fails after a top-up purchase, the Play Billing token
is intentionally not consumed (fail-safe keeps e-coins recoverable).
Until now there was no retry path: the user hit itemAlreadyOwned on the
next buy and had to wait ~3 days for Google's auto-refund.

On onBillingSetupFinished, also query INAPP purchases and, for any
PURCHASED top-up whose productId is in TOPUP_PRODUCT_IDS, re-run
consumeAndVerifyTopup.

fix(deploy-hetzner): whitelist operator IP in fail2ban

Ubuntu 24.04 cloud-init enables fail2ban with default sshd jail
(5 retries → 10m ban, escalating on repeat). Local proxies like
ClashX in global mode race TCP handshakes through a tunnel exit
and can trigger "connection closed pre-auth" on sshd's side even
for legitimate operator connections. The operator then gets
DROP-ruled off their own VM, can't SSH in to fix it, and has to
either wait out the ban or fall back to Hetzner's web console.

the job-level `if: ${{ vars.X != '' || secrets.X != '' }}` gate —

fix: abort provisioning if claw deleted mid-flight (race condition)

When an issue is immediately moved back out of trigger status, the
factory sets status='deleted' but the provisioning goroutine is already
running. Without this fix the VM would finish provisioning, overwrite
'deleted' with 'starting', broadcast claw_status, and the card would
reappear on the dashboard.

Fix:
- Check status='deleted' at goroutine start before calling provisionReplicated
- Guard all Replicated status='starting'

Fix SQLite timestamp format mismatch in factory event pruning

Add _time_format=sqlite to the SQLite connection string to ensure
time.Time values are stored in SQLite's native datetime format
(YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS) instead of Go's RFC3339 format. This fixes
the event pruning query in logFactoryEvent() which compares
created_at timestamps against datetime('now', '-4 hours').

Without this fix, timestamps stored with 'T' separator were
lexicographically greater than SQLite's space-separated format,
Add Feishu-optimized fixed PRD template

Fix Ollama subprocess: add stdin=DEVNULL to prevent hanging

Both _llm_distill_batch() and _llm_distill_single() were hanging because
subprocess.run was inheriting stdin, causing Ollama to wait for input.

Adding stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL closes the input stream, allowing Ollama
to complete normally.

The code_quality collector was counting bare TODO/FIXME occurrences
anywhere in .js/.ts/.py files, which inflated the deferred-work signal
with:

- its own docstring ("TODO count" as a metric name)
- regex patterns in security/token-efficiency scanners (r'TODO',
  r'FIXME')
- test fixture strings and test inputs that happen to contain "TODO"
- duplicates from graphify-pilot/ (snapshot, not live code)

fix(csp): drop upgrade-insecure-requests to unblock LAN deployments

Helmet's default CSP bundle includes `upgrade-insecure-requests`, which
browsers enforce on private IPv4 ranges (10.x / 172.16-31.x / 192.168.x)
but relax on localhost. On an HTTP LAN host the JS bundle request was
rewritten to https://<lan-ip>:3001/assets/*.js, failed with no TLS, and
Solid never mounted — users reported a blank page. Disabling the
directive is safe here because every fetch directive is already scoped
to 'self

fix(config): UI 保存即时生效——消除 AppState::config 与 cached_config 分叉

根因：AppState::config: Mutex<AppConfig> 与 cached_config() ArcSwap
并存，save_*_config 命令只刷新 cached_config 不写 Mutex，chat.rs
热路径读 Mutex 永远是启动快照，表现为"UI 启用 image_generate 不生效，
重启才行"。

方案：删除 AppState::config 字段，单一真相源：
- 读统一走 ha_core::config::cached_config()（ArcSwap lock-free 快照）
- 写统一走 ha_core::config::mutate_config((category, source), |cfg|...)
  进程全局写锁 + 读最新快照 + mutate + 持久化 + 原子 swap + emit
  config:changed 事件
- save_config 内部自动发 EventBus

feat(audio-transcriber): detect language before VAD to prevent misdetection

auto language detection gets confused by silence → detects Hebrew, English etc.

BEFORE VAD, then lock the result into decode_options={language: ...} for
the main transcription call. This prevents silence from ever influencing
language detection.

   client. Nothing appeared until the server's 30s idle-timeout ended
   the stream and t

feat(core): add CapabilityRegistry (H5)

Single differentiation point between Community and Enterprise editions.
Community ships with a default registry that reports zero enterprise

fix: preserve in-flight optimistic messages when loadMessages races with send

loadMessages() was unconditionally replacing messages[clawId] with the
fresh API result. If it ran while send() was awaiting apiSendMessage(),
the opt- prefixed optimistic message was wiped before send() could replace
it with the real UUID, silently dropping the user message.

Fix: re-append any opt- messages from existing state that aren't already
covered by a matching message in the API response.

fix(compression): exclude completion tokens from compression trigger (#12026)

Cherry-picked from PR #12481 by @Sanjays2402.

Reasoning models (GLM-5.1, QwQ, DeepSeek R1) inflate completion_tokens
with internal thinking tokens. The compression trigger summed
prompt_tokens + completion_tokens, causing premature compression at ~42%
actual context usage instead of the configured 50% threshold.

Now uses only prompt_tokens — completion tokens don't consume context
window space for the next API call.

public browser-nav

fix(session): 未读徽标只计 assistant 行，不再算工具/思考/事件

list_sessions_paged / get_session 的未读子查询原本用 role != 'user'，把
tool / text_block / thinking_block / event 这些一轮 turn 的组件与系统事件
全部算进未读。一个问题触发 10 次工具调用就会显示 20+ 红点，误导用户以为
有成堆漏看的回复。改为 role = 'assistant'，一轮 turn = 1 条未读，Cron
连续触发 N 次 = N 条未读，语义与用户直觉一致。unread_count 是动态子查询
无需迁移；索引扫描路径不变，= 比 != 选择性更高。

fix(chat): 切换会话时重置 Canvas 面板最大化/分离态

CanvasPanel 的 maximized / detached 是组件本地 state，组件挂在
ChatScreen 顶层自始至终存活，切会话只会刷新 canvas 数据、不会清
视图态。结果是：在会话 A 把 canvas 最大化过后切到同样有 canvas 的
历史会话 B，面板继续以 fixed inset-0 z-50 覆盖整个聊天区，消息看
起来"全不见了"；detached 窗口同理会留在屏幕上指向旧 project 的
iframe。currentSessionId 切换的 effect 里加一次 setMaximized(false)
+ 关闭并清空 detachedWindowRef，从根上避免上一个会话的视图态渗漏
到新会话。

fix(ci): add safe.directory for container jobs (gix ownership check)

Container-based CI jobs run as root but the workspace is bind-mounted
from the host runner, so files are owned by a different uid. The
checkout action's safe.directory config is written to a temporary HOME
that doesn't persist, causing gix::discover() to reject the repo.

Add an explicit safe.directory step after checkout in all three
container jobs (ci.yml Rust CI, ci.yml Full Targets, release.yml
Clippy).

Tray: add Open Control UI via SSH port-forward tunnel

Adds a new tray menu item that opens the OpenClaw Control UI in the
default browser over an SSH tunnel to the Mac. First click spawns
`ssh -N -L 18790:127.0.0.1:18789 <user>@<host>` (with
ExitOnForwardFailure=yes + ServerAliveInterval=30), waits for the
local port to accept TCP, then opens http://localhost:18790/#token=<token>
using the DPAPI-stored gateway token. Subsequent clicks reuse the
open handle; a crashed ssh is detected and re-spaw

chore(openclaw-channel): log non-final reply chunks being skipped

Without this, a silent `return` makes it impossible to tell apart
"agent said nothing" from "agent replied but we filtered it out"
(e.g. if OpenClaw renames the `final` kind in a future release, every
reply would just vanish with zero trace). Log line includes kind,
text length, and media count so the signal is useful but never
exposes message content.

fix(portal): drop duplicate fmtAgo — blanked whole Vaults view

portal-v2.html already defined fmtAgo at line 587. I redeclared it at
line 3045 next to fmtExpiry for symmetry. Babel's JSX transform
compiles in strict mode, and strict mode throws on duplicate
function declarations — the whole Babel script failed to parse, so
React never mounted and /v2 served a blank page.

Keep the earlier fmtAgo; only fmtExpiry is new (not a duplicate).

each relayed as a separate IM message).

strictly correct and subsumes the old `tool

fix(windows): run frontend build before Pack; disable beforeBuildCommand

Root cause: the prebuild hook (in package.json "prebuild" script) runs
verify-clawdbot-bundle.cjs, which calls process.exit(1) when node_modules
is missing. After the Pack step removes node_modules, Tauri's
beforeBuildCommand ("cd .. && npm run build") triggers the prebuild hook
again, causing an immediate failure (~3s exit).

Fix:
1. Move "Build frontend" (npm run build) to BEFORE the Pack step so
   node_modules is still

fix(openclaw-channel): thread agent-scoped media roots into reply dispatch

dispatchReplyWithBufferedBlockDispatcher bypasses the outbound adapter,
so core never injects mediaLocalRoots for us — loadWebMedia falls back
to getDefaultLocalRoots(), which does NOT cover a non-default agent's
workspace. Any MEDIA: absolute path under ~/.openclaw/workspace-<id>/…
therefore gets rejected as path-not-allowed, and relative paths anchor
to the default agent's workspace instead of the caller's.

fix(sessions): surface compression tips in session lists and resume lookups (#12960)

After a conversation gets compressed, run_agent's _compress_context ends
the parent session and creates a continuation child with the same logical
conversation. Every list affordance in the codebase (list_sessions_rich
with its default include_children=False, plus the CLI/TUI/gateway/ACP
surfaces on top of it) hid those children, and resume-by-ID on the old
root landed on a dead parent with no messages.

fix(windows): pack clawdbot node_modules into tar before WiX build

WiX candle.exe generates a .wxs XML entry for every resource file.
With ~13k node_modules files the XML grows to ~50 MB and OOMs candle.
Pack node_modules into a single node_modules.tar first so WiX sees
~3k files instead of ~16k, keeping candle within memory limits.

fix(codex): surface auth failures instead of silent "exit status 1" loop (#158)

When codex can't authenticate (no `auth.json`, expired ChatGPT OAuth, no
OPENAI_API_KEY), it retries 5x against `wss://api.openai.com/v1/responses`,
hits 401, and exits 1. WUPHF logs the wrapped err as
`error: exit status 1: exit status 1`, the channel shows "Routing to @X..."
forever, and there is no way for the user to tell what happened.

Three fixes:

1. `preflightHeadlessCodexAuth` — runs before spawning codex.

fix(scan_environment): expand scan_credentials canonical paths (F7)

Pre-F7 scan_credentials() only scanned ~/.agents/.env.global, ~/.env*,
and ~/.agents/secrets/**/*.env. On a fresh user install, ClawSeat seat
secrets (MiniMax API key etc.) live at ~/.agent-runtime/secrets/ which
was never scanned — the ancestor would report 'no credentials found'
even when the machine was correctly provisioned.

Expanded to 6 canonical directories (all rglob *.env):
  ~/.agents/secrets             (existing)

perf: optimize tool name resolution with Map-based caching

introducing a `Map` to cache canonicalized tool names. This reduces the
lookup complexity from O(N) to O(1) per lookup.

- Refactored `resolveKnownToolName` and `resolveKnownTool` to accept an optional `toolMap`.
